name: White Chocolate Mocha
directions: Add milk and chocolate chips to saucepan and heat until chips are melted.
Meanwhile make coffee in espresso machine.
Pour half of milk mixture in coffee mug and fill rest of mug with coffee saving enough room for a dollop of whipped cream.
Enough for 2 mugs.

name: Iced Tea
directions: Make very strong tea in a large teapot using the tea-bags and 1 liter/quart boiling water.
Add the honey and 6 sprigs of the mint to the teapot
After 20 minutes remove the tea-bags, and let the tea get really cold.
When the tea is cold remove the mint.
Put ice in a large jug, pour the tea over, then the orange juice and the juice of the lime.
Follow this with the other sprigs of mint, the orange and lemon slices.
Stir well and serve in glasses with more ice.

name: Blue Heaven
directions: Add all ingredients to a cocktail shaker filled with ice.
Shake vigorously.
Strain into an ice-filled highball glass.
Garnish with maraschino cherry and pineapple wedge.

name: Olive Garden Sangria
directions: This makes a gallon.
Mix all the ingredients (i used a plastic ice cream bucket), except for ice.
Let sit in refrigerator atleast 4-5 hours, if not overnight.
Pour sangria into serving glass, fruit and all, then add ice.

name: Irish Spring
directions: Pour all ingredients, except orange slice and cherry, over crushed ice in a 10 ounce glass.
Stir well.
Garnish with the orange slice and cherry.

name: Iced Nutty Butterscotch Coffee
directions: Mix all ingredients together and pour over ice.
Serve.

name: Cappuccino
directions: Using an espresso machine or cappuccino maker, brew 2 shots of espresso.
Steam 1 cup milk, generating a good amount of thick froth for crema.
Divide espresso into 2 serving mugs; divide steamed milk between the 2 as well, then top each with half of the thick foam (Ideal ratio is 1/3 espresso, 1/3 milk, 1/3 crema).
Dust with cinnamon and/or freshly grated nutmeg, if desired& serve.

name: Raspberry Martini
directions: In a shaker shake all ingredients till well chilled.
Strain into chilled martini glasses.
Garnish with a fresh raspberry.
Cheers!

name: Spiced Lemon and Lime Ade
directions: In a medium saucepan combine the water, the rosemary, and the allspice.
Bring to boiling.
Remove from heat.
Cover and let stand for 1 hour or until cool.
Remove two of the rosemary sprigs; wrap, and chill to use later for garnishing the pitcher.
Strain liquid through a fine sieve, discarding solids.
Stir sugar into strained liquid until dissolved.
Cover and chill 1 to 24 hours.
To serve, in large pitcher combine herb-spice liquid, lemon and lime juices, and cold water.
Float lemon and/or lime slices and reserved rosemary sprigs in pitcher.
Serve in tall glasses over ice.
Makes seven 12-ounce servings.
Enjoy!
Note: cook time is just the time it takes to boil the water, it may vary.

name: Cran-fizzy Iced Tea
directions: Steep 4 Tea bags in 2 cups boiling water according to package directions.
Discard Tea bags.
Stir in can of frozen cranberry juice concentrate.
Cover and chill.
To serve, fill a glass of ice one-quarter full with tea mixture.
Top off with chilled Club Soda.
You may also add fresh berries as a garnish.

name: Tropical Sparkling Sangria
directions: Reserve some for the lime and carambolas slices for garnish.
In a large pitcher, stir together the white wine, the mango nectar, the mango and the remaining lime and carambola slices.
The sangria can be prepared up to this point one day in advance and kept covered and chilled.
Just before serving, stir in the ginger ale, the champagne and ice cubes.
Serve the sangria garnished with the reserved lime and carambola slices.

name: Gingerbread Latte
directions: Measure syrup into serving mug or cup.
Brew espresso, and steam the milk, producing a small amount of frothed milk, or crema.
Add espresso to mug or cup, then steamed milk, and top with froth/crema (or whipped cream, if desired).
Sprinkle lightly with ground cinnamon and serve.

name: Mango Cosmopolitan
directions: Shake in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Strain into a martini glass and garnish with a lime wedge, if desired.

name: Soft Breeze
directions: Pour in a highball glass over ice.
Garnish with a lime wedge.

name: Springly Smash (non-alcoholic)
directions: Combine juices and sugar in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add mint leaves and cool.
Strain into a highball or collins glass over ice. Add soda to fill.
Garnish with mint.

name: Sunny Swizzle (non-alcoholic)
directions: Combine the juices, sugar and bitters in a shaker half filled with ice. Shake well.
Strain into a collins glass almost filled with crushed ice. Stir.
Fill with soda. Serve with a swizzle stick.

name: Celibate Sling
directions: Pour cherry soda, ginger ale and lemon juice in a collins glass. Stir.
Float grenadine on top.
Garnish with fruit and serve with a straw.

name: Pumpkin Pie Coffee Creamer
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Shake to blend thoroughly.
Stir into hot coffee.

name: Low Carb Irish Cream
directions: Mix cocoa and coffee powders with Splenda.
Wet mixture with a bit of the whiskey to make a smooth paste.
Meanwhile, place cream in a heavy pan and cook on LOW heat until reduced to 2 1/2 cups.
Cool cream to room temperature.
Blend whiskey, paste, extracts and cream until smooth.

name: Baybreeze Mojito
directions: Muddle pineapple and mint leaves in bottom of glass. Use a muddler, the flat end of a cooking spoon, etc.
Add ice to fill glass.
Add rest of ingredients, stir, and serve.

name: Lemon Cooler
directions: mix all ingredients in a glass, over ice, drink up!
For a little boost, place a mint sprig in the glass before adding the liquids.  YUMMY.

name: Avocado Daiquiri
directions: Crush ice in blender.
Add rum, lime juice, and 1/4 medium avocado in blender.
Blend until thoroughly mixed and smooth.
Pour into glass and garnish with lime wedge.

name: Summertime Irish Cream Drink
directions: In a blender, combine all ingredients, and blend until smooth (about 30 seconds).
Pour into fancy serving glasses with lots of ice.
Delicious!

name: Latte Miel
directions: In a latte cup, stir together honey and remaining ingredients.
I do not have an espresso maker, so I brew strong coffee and heat the milk and honey until frothy in my Mochalatte appliance.
Timing depends on your coffee appliance.

name: Hot Spiced Tea
directions: Add spices to water and bring to a rolling boil.
Add tea bags, cover, and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain into heated teapot or carafe.
Heat juices and sugar just to boiling.
Add to hot tea.

name: Iced Tea Slush
directions: 1.  Mix all together and freeze.
2.  To serve, fill a glass half full and top with lemon-lime soda.
3.   Cheers!
4.  I'm guessing on the number of servings -- maybe you have really big glasses!

name: Mexican Coffee
directions: In an electric pot mix all the ground coffee with the 2 teaspoons of cinnamon in the filter basket.
Add water and brew as usual.
In a one-quart saucepan, mix together milk, chocolate syrup, and sugar over low heat until the sugar dissolves.
Add this to the carafe of brewed coffee then stir in the vanilla.
Pour into preheated cups and garnish, as desired, with whipped cream and a dash of cinnamon.
(Note, use brown sugar because it enhances the flavors of both the chocolate and the coffee, much preferable over white sugar).

name: Buttered Coffee
directions: In a tall glass fill with ice.
Add liqueurs and milk. Stir.
Top with whipped cream and swirl some syrup on top.

name: Banana Nut Bread Coffee
directions: Pour 1/2 of the liquors into two coffee cups, stir in sugar if wanted.
Topping with hot coffee.
Garnish with whipped cream and cinnamon.

name: Gimme My Mojito!
directions: In a tall thin glass, crush part of the mint with a fork to coat the inside.
Add the sugar and lemon juice and stir thoroughly.
Top with ice.
Add rum and mix. Top off with the club soda (or seltzer).
Add a lemon slice and the remaining mint.

name: Champagne Lemonade
directions: Put sugar and 1 cup water into a small saucepan and brin to a boil. Cook for 1 minute until sugar has dissolved. Turn off heat and allow sugar syrup to cool.
In a pitcher, combine cooled syrup with lemon juice and remaining 2 cups of water and mix well. Fill a Champagne flute halfway with the lemonade and top with champagne. Garnish with a lemon slice.

name: Coffee Royal
directions: In a coffee mug, disslove sugar in a small amount of hot coffee.
Add brandy or bourbon.
Fill with hot coffee.
Stir, and top with whipped cream.

name: Espresso Martini
directions: Combine ingredients with ice and shake in a shaker.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with a hershey's kiss.

name: Chocolate Iced Coffee
directions: Bring water and coffee to a boil in a saucepan.
Remove from heat and let stand 10 minutes.
Pour mixture through a fine wire-mesh strainer into a bowl, discarding coffee grounds.
Stir sugar and cinnamon into coffee until sugar dissolves.
Let cool.
Stir in half and half and chocolate syrup.
Chill 2 hours.
Serve over ice.
Note:  To keep coffee from diluting, freeze cubes of half and half and coffee to use in place of ice.

name: Iced Coffee Latte
directions: Place coffee in filter in brew basket of coffee maker. Add water to coffee maker; brew.
Cool completely.  Pour coffee into pitcher.  Add milk; stir until well blended.  Serve immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.
Serve over ice cubes.  Add sugar to taste, if desired.

name: Chocolate Kiss
directions: Combine liquor with 2 scoops of vanilla ice cream.
Blend with ice and garnish with chocolate sprinkles.

name: Vanillatini
directions: Dip frozen martini glasses in water and then in vanilla sugar.
Measure 4 ounces (1/2 cup) Vanilla Vodka that has been kept in the freezer into a cocktail shaker that is half full of ice and shake 10 times.
Add triple sec and swirl a few times then  strain into prepared glass.
Squeeze orange slice to release juice and drop into glass.
Serve.

name: Tropical Passion
directions: Combine all ingredients and shake with ice.
Strain into highball glass.

name: Aloha Martini
directions: Shake with ice.
Strain into a cocktail glass and enjoy!

name: Starbucks Marble Mocha Macchiato
directions: Steam milk until it reaches 145 degrees F.
Pour white chocolate syrup into bottom of a coffee mug, followed by freshly brewed espresso.
Pour in steamed milk.
Top with Hershey's syrup.
Stir.
Enjoy!

name: Champagne Mojito
directions: Muddle mint, sugar, lime wedge and lime juice with a muddler or the back of a spoon until sugar dissolves, about 30 seconds.
Strain into a chilled champagne glass.
Add chilled Champagne and garnish with a spring of mint.

name: Guava Mojito
directions: Muddle mint springs and sugar together in a measuring cup. Use this cup to measure the rum and juices so you rinse out all the yummy goodness.
Place muddled mint and sugar in cocktail shaker half filled with ice, add rum and juices and shake 15 times.
Strain into glass filled with ice. Dilute to desired strength with club soda. optional.
Garnish with mint.

name: Sunrise
directions: Add 3-4 ice cubes in an oversize wineglass.
Pour the tangerine juice over the ice; top with pomegranate jucie, which will sink in slowly.
Serve garnished with lime.

name: Pomegranate Cocktail
directions: Combine all ingredients and serve in ice filled glasses.

name: Trader Vic's Grog
directions: Place in shaker.
Shake  and pour into an old fashioned glass.
Decorate with a cherry.  Serve with straw.
Note: Trader Vics served this in a  ten pin pilsener glass.

name: Trader Vic's Caribbean
directions: Place in shake with cracked ice.
Garnish with slice orange and pineapple decoration.
Add a sprig of mint
serve with long straw.

name: Trader Vic's Babalu
directions: Blend.
Serve in a 10 ounce glass.
Garnish with mint and a fruit stick.
[take a skewer and put your favorite fancy cut fruit on it].
Serve with malt straw.

name: Chai Tea
directions: Crush the cardamom pods with the side of a knife and place in a saucepan along with the ginger, cinnamon, cloves and peppercorns.  Add water and set aside for 30 minutes.
Bring to a rolling boil.  Remove from heat and add tea bags and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain, pressing spices and tea bags with a spoon to remove the rich liquid.  Return liquid to pot, add milk and bring to a boil.  Immediately remove from heat.
Add honey or sugar to taste and  than return to boil twice more, allowing a minute of cooling between each boil.
Serve hot in tea mugs or cups that have been warmed.  Makes 6 cups.

name: Spiced Iced Coffee
directions: Combine first 6 ingredients; stir well. Let stand at room temperature 1 hour.
Strain and serve over cracked ice. Serve with half-and-half and sugar.

name: White Sangria
directions: Dissolve in a Pitcher the water and sugar.
Add; Chill Two Hours the wine, brandy vanilla bean, lime, orange and pineapple.
Stir in club soda, cubed ice.
Pour into Glasses; Garnish with, Fresh mint sprigs and Fresh raspberries.

name: Hibiscus Orange Tea
directions: Bring water to  boil, turn off heat add the hibiscus flowers and cinnamon sticks.
Cover and steep for about 20 minutes.
Strain tea into a pitcher, add sugar to taste.
Add orange slices and serve over ice.
Garnish each glass with a lemon slice and serve. Enjoy!

name: Three-Minute Caramel Latte
directions: Microwave evaporated milk and ice cream topping in small, uncovered microwave-safe bowl on HIGH (100%) power for 2 minutes or until very hot.
Carefully pour mixture into blender, cover and blend on high for 1 minute or until very frothy on top.
Place 2 teaspoons coffee granules into each 12-ounce coffee mug.
Add 1/2 cup hot water to each mug and stir.
Gently pour milk mixture into each mug, spooning foam on top.
Thinly drizzle foam with ice cream topping, if desired.

name: Cafe Orange Mocha
directions: Mix coffee and hot chocolate.
Place one orange slice into each cup.
Pour coffee mixture into cups.
Top with whipped cream, and garnish with cinnamon.

name: Coffee Grog, Non Alcoholic
directions: Place one piece of each peel into cups.
Mix butter, sugar, cloves, nutmeg and cinnamon.
Mix coffee and cream.
Pour both mixtures into cups and stir.
*NOTE: For the strips of peel I use a veggie peeler and take a good sized strip from the fruits trying NOT to include the white part.

name: Pomegranate Mojito Virgin
directions: Muddle mint with a splash of simple syrup and three lime wedges in a glass.
Fill glass with ice. Pour in pomegranate juice and soda.
Garnish with lime wedge and a sprig of mint.
ENJOY!

name: Homemade Sangria
directions: Mix in all the ingredients except the Ginger Ale in a Sangria Pitcher.
Let the flavours to combine for several hours in the fridge.  24 hours if you can.
At the end you can add ice cubes if you want.  But I don't.
Pour in each glass.  Add a little bit of Ginger Ale.  Decorate with slices of oranges and a marashino cherry.
If the sangria is too strong, you can add a little bit of juice of your choice.

name: French Kiss
directions: Pour equal parts Pernod, vodka, and lemonade into a tall glass over ice.
Add a splash of black currant liqueur.
Garnish with a slice of lemon, a few whole cranberries or currants (if available), and/or lightly coat rim with sugar - lightly toasted if desired.
Enjoy!

name: Merry Mimosas
directions: Put sugar and orange juice in a blender.  Cover and blend on medium speed about 15 seconds, or until foamy.
Put 2 Tbsp cranberry juice in each of 8 glasses.
Pour 1/4 c orange juice into each glass.
Add about 1 c champagne (or grape juice) to each glass.
Garnish with orange slices.

name: Golden Cadillac
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender and blend for 10 seconds.
Strain into cocktail glasses.

name: Bailey's Snowball
directions: Combine ingredients in a shaker of ice.
Pour into a martini glass; garnish.
Enjoy!  ;).

name: Bailey's Espresso Martini
directions: Add all ingredients to a shaker of ice; shake.
Pour into martini glass; garnish.
Enjoy! ;).

name: Bailey's Raspberry Martini
directions: Combine ingredients in a shaker of ice; shake.
Pour into a martini glass; garnish with a raspberry.
Enjoy!  ;) .

name: Midnight Mint
directions: Shake ingredients together in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Strain into a cocktail glass.

name: White-Out
directions: Mix all ingredients together, except cream. to be shaken, not stirred
pour into a tumbler glass (I use a stemless white wine glass --
add a splash of cream. Stir at this point.
Add a few ice cubes and enjoy!

name: Mock Champagne
directions: Combine in a large punch bowl.
Serve chilled.

name: Cafe Mystique
directions: Blend all ingredients in the blender.  Add brown sugar if using bourbon and not Kahlua.
Fill glasses with ice and pour over the cubes.
Top with whipped cream if desired.

name: Cheery, Cherry, Cinnamon Tea
directions: Mix all together and put in an airtight container.
When ready to serve stir in 1-2 tablespoons in a cup of hot water and stir well.

name: Caribbean Romance
directions: Shake all ingredients well with ice and pour into highball glass. Float grenadine on top and garnish with an orange, lemon, or lime slice.

name: Mariposa
directions: Shake all ingredients together with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Tropical Cocktail
directions: Mix ingredients in tall glass filled with ice cubes. Garnish with a pineapple wedge.

name: Bailey's Banana Colada
directions: Half fill blender with ice. Combine ingredients & blend until ice is crushed & mixture is smooth.
Pour into tall glasses, garnish and enjoy!

name: Iced Sunrise
directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a pitcher, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Chill. Serve over ice; garnish with lime slices, if desired.

name: Jasmine Fancy Limeade Iced Tea
directions: Boil water and let tea steep at least 15 minutes.
Mix with 2-3 cups limeade.
Pour into pitcher with ice.

name: Heavenly Sangria
directions: Squeeze 2 each oranges, lemons, limes.  Mix with wine, sugar, brandy and assorted fruit.  Let rest in refrigerator overnight.  Before serving, add the ginger ale and slice remaining orange, lemon, lime and add for color and decoration.  Serve over ice!

name: White Sangria
directions: In a pitcher, blend all ingredients, except the tonic water and ice cubes.
Let cool 1 hour in the fridge.
Add tonic water, ice cubes and mint when ready to serve.

name: The Bubbly Melon
directions: Combine in an ice-filled shaker and strain into a martini glass.
Top with 1/2 ounce of your favorite Champagne.
ENJOY!  ;).

name: Red Moon
directions: Pour in a tall glass of ice.
Garnish with a slice of orange and enjoy!

name: Tropical Paradise
directions: blend with ice and pour into a 5oz cocktial or martini glass and serve.
Garnish with an orchid, or slice of fresh mango.
Enjoy!  ;).

name: Nutcracker
directions: Begin by muddling the mint in a shaker; add remaining ingredients with a small amount of ice.
Shake all ingredients and pour over ice in a tall (8oz) glass.
Garnish with a slice of apple and enjoy!  ;).

name: Tauris
directions: Muddle the mint and sugar in a shaker,add the remaining ingredients and shake vigorously.
Pour ALL into an old fashioned glass and serve.

name: Bubbletart (Non-Alcoholic)
directions: Shake juices with ice and strain into a chilled highball glass.
Fill with mineral water.
Garnish with a lime wheel.

name: Russian Tea
directions: Bring water to a boil and turn off heat.
Add tea bags and spices.
Cover and leave for 5 minutes.
Remove tea bags and spices from water.
Add sugar and juices.
Mix well and reheat.

name: Bella Peach
directions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker and strain over crushed ice.
Garnish with fresh raspberries if you're out to impress a crowd, or simply want to indulge your inner high-roller!

name: Pink Flirtini
directions: Muddle the raspberries in the bottom of a martini glass.
Combine the raspberry vodka, Cointreau, pineapple juice, cranberry juice and lime juice in a shaker with ice.
Shake and strain into glass. Top with Pink. Garnish with a sprig of mint.

name: Pomegranate Blush
directions: Add Pama Liquor, Cointreau and Orange Juice in a shaker with ice.
Shake to chill.
Pour into flutes and add champagne.
Garnish with Orange wheel and serve.

name: Gingerbread Coffee
directions: In a small bowl, mix together molasses, brown sugar, baking soda, ginger and cinnamon until well blended.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes.
Add about 1/4 cup coffee to each of the 6 cups.
Stir about a tablesoon of the spice mixture intor into each cup until dissolved.
Fill each cup to within an inch of the top with coffee.
Stir in half and half to taste and garnish with whipped cream and ground cloves.

name: Malibu Mango Mojito
directions: Muddle mint leaves and 1 lime wedge in the bottom of tall glass.
Add rum, club soda, ice, and lime wedges.
Garnish with mint and enjoy this tasty treat!

name: St. Germain Can-Can Martini
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice and fine strain into a chilled Martini glass.
Garnish with lemon or orange twist.

name: Coconut Cream Mocha Espresso
directions: Mix heavy cream and coconut milk in small glass.  Set aside.
In a small microwave safe serving glass, combine the Irish Cream and chocolate liqueur.
Heat for one minute in microwave or until hot.
Add espresso and stir carefully.
Using a spoon held upside down, slowly pour the cream over the back of the spoon into the serving glass.  This will let you float the cream on top.
Dust with cocoa powder if you want.  It will give it that finishing touch but is not necessary.

name: Canadian Mojito
directions: In cocktail shaker, combine mint leaves, blueberries and lime juice; muddle. Add lemonade and rye; top with ice cubes. Shake well. Strain into old-fashioned glass. Add splash of soda water.

name: Breakfast Mocha 2
directions: Mix the first 4 ingredients; gradually stir in water.
Heat through.
Float small scoopful of ice cream on top.
Serves 4.

name: Hibiscus Tea
directions: Combine water and ginger in a large pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Remove from heat and stir in Jamaica flowers and sugar until sugar is dissolved. Let steep 10 minutes.
Strain through a chinois 'china cap' or fine mesh sieve into a large, heat-resistant bowl or pot. Stir in lime juice and set aside to cool. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Serve over ice.

name: Cranberry  Sparkler
directions: In a pitcher, mix all ingredients.
Serve over ice and garnish with an orange wedge.

name: Mexican Caramel Coffee
directions: Mix all ingredients in a mug and enjoy!

name: Dark Chocolate Latte
directions: Stir milk, half and half, dark chocolate chips and instant coffee granules together in a small saucepan over low heat until dark chocolate chips are melted.
Stir in vanilla and almond extracts; pour evenly into 4 mugs. Sweeten to taste.
Top with whipped cream, if desired.
Garnish, if desired, with a cinnamon stick,  and serve immediately.
Enjoy!

name: Gingerbread Latte
directions: DIRECTIONS.
In a coffee mug, combine espresso coffee with flavored syrup. Pour in steamed milk. Top with whipped cream, and sprinkle with nutmeg, cinnamon, and vanilla powder.

name: Sunflower Martini
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice and serve in martini glass.
Garnish with a rose.
Enjoy!  :).

name: Sin City Cosmopolitan
directions: Add ice and shake for 15 seconds.
Strain into a martini glass.
Garnish with an orange spiral.
Enjoy!  :).

name: Mango Tango
directions: Pour all in highball glass with lots of ice adding mint leaves.
Garnish with sprig of Mint.

name: Vanilla Lips
directions: Shake with ice and serve in Collins glass.
Garnish with cherry and orange slice, enjoy!  :).

name: Sweet Kiss
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice.
Serve in Rio Grande glass; garnish with a lime slice and/or red currant cluster.
Enjoy!  :).

name: Black Cherry Iced Tea
directions: Bring water to boil in a medium-sized pot.
Add cherries, sugar, lemon juice, and star anise.
Simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, until the cherries are almost falling apart.
Turn off heat, mix in black tea, and let stand for 1 hour to cool.
Strain liquid through a fine mesh sieve, mashing the cherries with a wooden spoon to extract more liquid.
Discard solids.
Transfer liquid to a pitcher and refridgerate until ready to serve.

name: Butterscotch Bombshell
directions: Shake vigorously with ice.
Strain into chilled martini glass.
Garnish with butterscotch biscuit or similar cookie.
Enjoy!  :).

name: Elixer
directions: Shake with ice, top with Cascade Cloudy Apple Juice.
Garnish with an apple slice, enjoy!  :).

name: Comfort and Cream
directions: Combine ingredients with ice in a shaker. Shake well and strain into a short glass or leave over ice.
Optional garnish: sprinkle with shaved chocolate or chocolate sprinkles. Also great rimmed with chocolate syrup!

name: Vampire's Kiss
directions: Put ice and water in martini glass, to chill.
Put all ingredients in cocktail shaker with ice and shake the heck out of it!
Dump ice/water from glass.
Strain into martini glass and ENJOY!

name: Hot Mulled  Decaf Tea
directions: Place a small amount of boiling water in the tea pot to warm it up. Discard the water.
Add tea bags, cinnamon stick, cloves and star anise to the tea pot and add the 4 cups  of boiling water.
Let steep for 3 to 5 minutes and remove the teabags.
Let steep for 2 minutes longer and serve immediately.
Sit back and enjoy!

name: Mojito Fruit Cocktail
directions: Mix all ingredients except garnish.
Let stand, tossing occasionally, 10 minutes until juicy and sugar dissolves.
To garnish and serve: spread about 2 Tbsp sugar on a plate.
Add 1 or 2 drops food coloring and blend with fingers until sugar is evenly tinted pink.
Pour alittle water in a shallow bowl, dip rim of martini glass in water then sugar to coat rim.
Spoon in fruit and juice.
Garnish with a lime slice.

name: Lemon Splash
directions: Single serving:
Fill a highball glass with ice.
Add Smirnoff Citrus Twist and lemonade.
Stir and add cranberry cocktail.
Top up with Perrier Lime and garnish with a twist of lemon.
Enjoy!
For a pitcher full of this refreshing drink:
Fill a large pitcher with ice.
Add a 12 ounce (341ml) can of frozen lemonade concentrate, 1 1/2 cups Smirnoff Citrus Twist, and 3 cups of Cranberry Cocktail.
Stir and put in refrigerator until guests arrive.
Remove from fridge and add 3 cups of Perrier Lime.
Add slices of fresh lemon or lime to the pitcher as a garnish and serve in tall glasses.

name: Intrigue Martini
directions: SHake ingredients well with ice.
Pour into a chilled martini glass.
Enjoy.

name: At Home Cappuccino
directions: Place milk in 2-cup glass measuring cup.  Heat in saucepan on stovetop until hot but not boiling.
Place hot milk and sugar in a blender container. Cover with a vented lid and blend until frothy, about 1 minute.
To serve, divide coffee among 4 cups. Top each with frothy milk. Sprinkle with cinnamon or grated chocolate. Sip and enjoy!

name: Caribbean Sunset
directions: Combine melon liqueur and malibu rum together in a hurricane glass.
Slowly pour cranberry juice on top until it fills half the remaining space.
Float orange juice on top, and serve.

name: Better Homes' Cappuccino
directions: *Note: amount of filtered water and coffee needed for this recipe is estimated depending on the type of coffeemaker you use.
Brew the coffee. If possible, please use filtered water to prepare the coffee.
In the meantime,  in a small chilled mixing bowl, (preferably metal) whip the heavy cream and powdered sugar together until soft peaks form-the tips will curl.
Pour the brewed coffee into cups.
Spoon the whipped cream onto the coffee. You don't have to stir the whipped cream as it will "melt" into the coffee from the heat.
Garnish with orange zest, if desired sprinkle with a little bit of cardamom and add one cinnamon stick per cup (the "stirrer" aka swizzle stick).
Each serving equals about 3 ounces.

name: Cruzan Cherry Mojito
directions: Muddle mint, lime and simple syrup in a highball.
Fill glass with crushed ice and add the Cruzan, topping with Soda.

name: Cherry Orchard
directions: Add all ingredients to a shaker of ice - shake ingredients and strain into a cocktail glass.
Garnish with apple slices.

name: Cruzan Rum Breeze
directions: Using a highball glass, add ice black cherry rum, light rum, pineapple juice and top with a splash of ginger ale.
Garnish with a twist of lime and enjoy!

name: White Sangria
directions: Combine water and sugar in a small saucepan.
Bring to a boil, stirring to dissolve sugar.  Remove from heat and let cool.
Combine fruit in a bowl.
In a large pitcher, combine sugar-water, wine, orange juice and amaretto.
Add the fruit and stir gently to combine.
Just before serving, add sparkling water to pitcher.
Serve sangria in wine glasses.

similar.name: Campari Orange Spritzer ( Ina Garten Back to Basics )

name: The Eastwick
directions: Combine ingredients over ice in a cocktail shaker.
Serve in chilled martini glass.
Customize your drink with a small scoop of passion fruit sorbet or a splash of ginger ale.

name: Lunartini
directions: Mix all in a shaker with ice.
Pour into a chilled martini glass.
Enjoy!

name: French Mojito
directions: Mix in a pint glass the Rum, Mint Leaves, Brown Sugar and juice from the Lime (quartered).
Muddle the ingredients until the mint leaves are pretty well broken up (approx 1-2 minutes).
Fill the pint glass, containing the muddled ingredients, with Ice.
Top off the pint glass with Sparkling Wine.
Transfer back and forth between the pint glass and a larger glass 3-4 times to mix.
Garnish with pressed lime quarters and serve.

name: Cold Brewed Jasmine Iced Tea
directions: In a large pitcher combine the tea and water. Mix the sugar or honey with about a cup of hot water so that it dissolves and will mix with the room temperature water. Add the sugar/ hot water mixture to the pitcher. Put the pitcher in the refrigerator (preferably before you go to sleep) and let the tea leaves soak until morning (9-12 hours). Add lemon to taste and then pour the tea through a strainer so as to catch the tea leaves before serving.

name: Irish Coffee
directions: Boil water and fill the glass mug to warm through. Dump out water.
Pour whiskey in glass, add coffee, sugar. Stir to combine.
Shake or vigorously stir heavy cream. Do NOT whip - the idea is to have frothy cream, not whipped stiff peaks. Gently float the cream on top of the coffee.
Optional: sprinkle nutmeg and/or cinammon on top.

name: Chocolate Cappuccino
directions: Combine liqueur and syrup in microwave-safe dish; heat on HIGH for 30 seconds.
Divide liqueur mixture among 8 mugs;add 1/4 cup espresso to each.
Froth milk; pour 1/4 cup milk into each mug.
Top with ground cinnamon; garnsh with chocolate shavings, if desired.

name: Campari Orange Spritzer ( Ina Garten Back to Basics )
directions: Place the ice cubes in a highball glass. Add the Campari, orange juice, and sparkling water. Add a slice of fresh orange and serve ice cold.
NOTE: Campari is a strong flavor. If you are more into sweetness substitute in ginger ale for the Pellegrino water to make it more to your taste.

name: Abilene
directions: Pour all ingredients over ice in a highball glass.  Mix well.
Enjoy.

name: Tea House Chai Concentrate
directions: Crush together the black pepper, ginger root, cardamom, cinnamon stick, and cloves in a coffee grinder or food processor(just pulse, do not grind to a powder).
Add to 8 cups of water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat  to medium and cook for 10 minutes.
Add 8 teaspoons of tea. Allen uses  Darjeeling OP. Steep for 8-10 minutes. Stain out the spices and tea. Add 1 teaspoons of vanilla and 3 teaspoons of honey, or sweeten to taste.  Enjoy!
This can be refrigerated for weeks.
Heat up as needed and add an equal amount of hot(Allen steams his) milk. Top with ground cinnamon.
Note:
For a mocha chai, use chocolate milk instead of white.
For herbal chai, replace the Darjeeling with 1 teaspoons each of chamomile, fennel seed and peppermint.

name: Delicious Iced Coffee
directions: Brew coffee strong. Transfer to heat proof container or cleaned milk jug. Store in freezer or refrigerator until cool.
Add milk, cocoa powder, and sugar to coffee. Shake well. Serve over ice.
Store in refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Instant cocoa mix can be subsituted for cocoa powder.
For frozen coffee drink, add ice and whip in blender.

name: Cinnamon-Caramel Iced Coffee
directions: Place coffee grounds inside paper filled (brewing) basket.
Add the cinnamon to the brewing basket.
Carefully stir the cinnamon and coffee grounds, to mix them together.
Adjust brewing basket to fit inside the coffee maker.
Pour the cold water inside coffee maker.
Place caramel ice cream topping, in empty pot of the coffee maker.
Start brewing the coffee.
When brewing is completed, remove coffee pot from the coffee maker.
Stir the coffee/caramel mixture, until it's well blended.
Set coffee pot aside, to cool the coffee.
Chill the coffee in the refrigerator, until ready to serve.
Pour chilled coffee in glasses filled with ice, and enjoy!

*Note: When making this coffee, the coffee I used was, Folger's Gourmet Selections Lively Columbian (Medium Roast). The caramel ice cream topping I used was, Smucker's, plus I used regular ice cubes rather than coffee flavored ice cubes. I actually prefered regular ice cubes in this coffee, as I just didn't care for the taste of the coffee flavored ice cubes in this coffee. One other thing I wanted to mention, there's no need to add sugar to the coffee, as the caramel topping gave this coffee just the right amount of sweetness.

name: Watermelon Gin Fizz
directions: Puree the watermelon. Strain (I do not strain, I simply use the puree); divide the juice among 4 TALL ice-filled glasses.
Top each glass with 1 1/2 ounces gin, 2 tablespoons lime juice and 1/3 cup ginger ale (I use a little more soda).
Garnish with the lime wedges.
NOTE:  I mix mine up to take to the pool by combining the puree, gin and lime juice in a container and then just take cans of soda.  I find that this amount makes 5 LARGE drinks.

name: Sweet Sun Tea
directions: Remove the paper tabs from tea bags.  Tie all the tea bag strings together, throw into glass container.
Add 1 cup sugar to glass container.  Fill container with water within 2" of top.  Shake container to dissolve the sugar.
Place in a sunny spot outside.
Do your chores or play, come back later, add ice cubes.
Pour into tall glass with slice of lemon or whatever.

name: Almond Mocha Coffee
directions: Whip heavy cream and 2 t of almond syrup in a small bowl with an electric mixer on low speed. After cream begins to thicken beat on high speed til its as thick as you like it. Refrigerate until you are ready to use it.
Pour milk in a medium microwave safe bowl. Heat on high heat for 1 to 1/2 minutes or until hot but not boiling. Whisk until foamy. Set aside.

Pour 2 tablespoons of almond syrup and 1 tablespoon of chocolate syrup into the bottom of each of 2 coffee cups. Microwave just long enough to warm syrups (about 20 seconds on high). Stir 1/4 cup hot coffee into each cup. Add steamed milk up to the top and the then garnish with almond whipped cream, nuts and cherries.

name: Brazilian Coffee
directions: Heat the chocolate, coffee and brandy or rum together. Fold in sweetened whipped cream or place a spoonful on top of each serving.  If desired, dust top with cinnamon.

name: Iced Tea Punch
directions: Make tea in regular manner. Add the 6 cups of sugar while tea is hot.
When cool, add 4 ingredients.
Mix well and put in tightly closed bottles or plastic cartons in refrigerator. Will keep several weeks if tightly closed:

name: Spiced Tea Mix
directions: FOR FULL RECIPE:
In a large bowl, combine all ingredients, and mix well.Store in an airtight container in a cool dry placefor up to 6 months.
TO PREPARE 1 GALLON ICED TEA:
Dissolve 2 cups of the tea mix in 1 gallon of water, and stir well.
Serve over ice.
16 servings.
TO PREPARE 1 CUP HOT TEA.
Dissolve 2 tablespoons of tea mix in 1 cup boiling water, and stir well.

name: Pampered Chef Sangria (Non-Alcoholic)
directions: Slice the oranges and lime.
Add the fruit, the frozen concentrates, and soda.
Mix well.  Add ice if desired.
* You may change the flavors of the frozen concentrates to get different results.  The tangier the concentrate mix, the tangier the sangria.
* The fruit makes a pretty presentation, but is not really required.

name: Chocolate Chai Tea
directions: In a saucepan, bring water to a boil. Remove from heat; add tea bags. Cover and steep 5 minutes. Remove tea bags.

Stir in sugar and cocoa. Bring to a boil; reduce heat. Add milk, vanilla, spices and salt. Cook and stir over medium heat until mixture is heated through, about 5 minutes. Do not boil. Strain, if desired. Pour into warm mugs. Garnish each with whipping cream and a sprinkle of nutmeg, if desired.

name: Spa Sangria
directions: In a large pitcher, combine wine and juiced cucumber. Stir in agave nectar and add slices of lemon, lime, orange, and diced mango. Refrigerate fro 24 - 48 hours before your gathering.
Fill 6 wine glasses with ice, and pour mixture over; float 1/2 oz orange liqueur in each, and garnish with additional orange wheels.

similar.name: Campari Orange Spritzer ( Ina Garten Back to Basics )

name: Berry Bubbles
directions: Blend berries, juice and sugar in blender till smooth.
place into wine glass and top up with chilled sparkling wine.

name: Citrus Blossom Matini
directions: Mix in shaker with cracked ice.  shake vigorously and strain into salt/sugar rimmed martini glass.

name: Bombay Sapphire Bramble
directions: Add the Bombay Sapphire, lemon juice and caster sugar to a short glass / tumbler and stir.
Fill the glass with crushed ice.
Drizzle the berry liqueur over the top and watch it 'marble' through the drink. Top up with more crushed ice and garnish with 1 blackberry and a wedge of fresh lemon.

name: St-Germain Rita
directions: Mix all ingredients, serve on ice.
You could also crush some ice in a blender and add the ingredients to make more of a typical frozen margarita type drink.
Please adjust the proportions to your own personal taste!

name: Peach and Green Apple Sangria
directions: Mix together the fruit and the sugar.  Let set for 30 min.in an extra large pitcher.  Then add all other ingredients and stir to combine.  Cover and chill for at least 4 hours Before serving.

name: Starbucks VIA® Instant Caramel Latte
directions: Tear open a single-serving packet.
Add 8 fl oz of hot water.
Stir and enjoy.

name: Starbucks® Pike Place® Roast
directions: Use cold, filtered water and a clean machine.
Grind beans just before brewing (Coarse for coffee press; Medium for automatic drip).
Measure 2 tbsp (10 g) ground coffee for every 6 fl oz (180 ml) water.

name: Starbucks® French Roast K-Cup®
directions: Drop the K-Cup pack into any K-Cup brewer.
Press brew.

name: Starbucks® Pike Place® Roast K-Cup®
directions: Drop the K-Cup pack into any K-Cup brewer.
Press brew.

name: Starbucks® French Roast
directions: Use cold, filtered water and a clean machine.
Grind beans just before brewing (Coarse for coffee press; Medium for automatic drip).
Measure 2 tbsp (10 g) ground coffee for every 6 fl oz (180 ml) water.

name: VODKa YUMMMOSA
directions: In a tall shaker glass, combine all the ingredients.  Place the stainless steel cup on top and shake the mixture for 20 to 30 seconds.
Remove the cup and place strainer over glass; pour into champagne glass or flute. Makes 1 cup.
